prompt: A detailed 19th-century naturalist scientific illustration of a sloping terrain ecosystem, rendered in the style of antique botanical plates. Multiple small organisms or plant tendrils are shown starting from scattered positions at higher elevations and gradually moving or growing downward toward a single fertile basin or water source. Each step of movement is subtly depicted as a sequence of positions, showing gradual convergence toward the lowest point. Fine ink linework, delicate cross-hatching, muted watercolor washes in sepia, olive, and faded greens. Paper texture aged and slightly yellowed. Composition resembles a scientific plate with careful observation of progression and variation. No modern elements, no text, no diagrams.
tradition_approach: Uses fine ink linework, cross-hatching, muted watercolor, and observational progression typical of naturalist plates to depict gradual convergence as a physical process.
